title: CLowl Message v0.2
description: CLowl (Claw + Talk) — a structured communication language for AI agent-to-agent messaging. Every message is auditable and has a deterministic English translation.

clowl.description: Protocol version. Must be '0.2' for this schema.

mid.description: Unique message ID. Use UUIDv7 (time-ordered). Enables idempotency and dedup.

ts.description: Unix epoch timestamp in seconds when the message was created.

tid.description: Trace ID. Links a task across multiple agents and hops. Set at task origin, propagate through all derived messages.

pid.description: Parent message ID. Links this message to the message it responds to. Null for root messages.

p.description: Performative code. Defines the intent of the message. REQ=Request, INF=Inform, ACK=Acknowledge, ERR=Error, DLGT=Delegate, DONE=Complete, CNCL=Cancel, QRY=Query, PROG=Progress, CAPS=Capabilities.

oneOf.description: Single recipient agent ID, or '*' for broadcast.
Array of recipient agent IDs for multicast.

to.description: Recipient(s). String, '*' for broadcast, or array for multicast.

cid.description: Conversation ID. Groups all related messages into a thread.

t.description: Free-form task type string. Examples: search, draft, analyze, review, deploy, notify. DLGT messages must include delegation_mode in body.d.

d.description: Task-specific data payload. Schema varies by task type. May be {} for simple ACK. DLGT messages must include 'delegation_mode' field with value: transfer, fork, or assist.

ref.description: File path or URL to shared context. Primary, token-efficient mode.

inline.description: Inline fallback content. Max 2000 characters. Use when ref is unreachable (cross-cloud, sandboxed agents).

hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the content at ref. Enables immutability verification.

ctx.description: Context reference object. ref is the preferred mode; inline is a degraded fallback.

auth.description: Optional auth token for sender identity verification and context access rights.

det.description: Determinism flag. true = agent declares result is reproducible (e.g., code execution, DB query). false (default) = LLM stochastic reasoning used.

delegation_mode.description: transfer = full handoff; fork = parallel work; assist = helper role.
